title: Record Vite React TypeScript npm scaffold decision
objective: Record the app scaffold decision for NoiseCut UI as Vite + React + TypeScript with npm, browser-safe API environment names, and implementation boundaries without creating runtime application files.

include: Create or update a durable planning artifact that records Vite + React + TypeScript as the selected scaffold.
Record npm as the package manager.
Record future env var names VITE_NOISECUT_API_BASE_URL and VITE_NOISECUT_API_TIMEOUT_MS.
Record future source root, test stack, and no-router default for v0.
Keep the work planning-only and ready for a later scaffold implementation task.
exclude: Running npm create vite or any scaffold generator.
Creating package.json, package-lock.json, Vite config, TypeScript config, src files, tests, or runtime source files.
Implementing React components, routing, API clients, or styles.
Choosing auth, billing, deployment, or backend-for-frontend behavior.

unknowns: Whether a future slice will need React Router after initial v0 flows are designed.
Whether deployment platform conventions require different public environment variable names.
Whether the scaffold implementation task should use Vite's generator or create files manually.
done: A durable planning artifact records Vite + React + TypeScript with npm as the selected scaffold.
The artifact records VITE_NOISECUT_API_BASE_URL and VITE_NOISECUT_API_TIMEOUT_MS as future browser-safe config names.
The artifact records src/ as the future source root and Vitest plus React Testing Library as the expected test stack.
No runtime app files, package files, API repo files, or extension repo files were changed.
Validation commands were run or skipped with a documented reason.
.ai/STATE.md was updated with task outcome and remaining risks.
If advanced state is in scope, .ai/state/STATE.md, .ai/state/snapshot.json, and .ai/state/events.jsonl were updated.
